other_observers: Mark Peterson - first visit Brandon Percival - second visit Kara Lewantowicz - third visit; Mark Peterson and I saw the bird at the same time.  It was first identified as a Slate-colored Fox Sparrow but on the second visit I could see that it was one of the red fox sparrows.
specific_location: East side of HQ Pond, Chico Basin Ranch
description: This is submitted as a record of a unusual (for CO) race of Red Fox Sparrow. subspecies unknown  Face with rufous auriculars on grayish background Tail and upper tail coverts rufous Lower rump rufous, upper rump medium gray Back - rufous on light gray background Wings - dark gray with rufous and faint whitish wingbars   Legs - pinkish  Bill - deep based pinkish, not yellowBehaviors: skulker flushed into open low perch then retreated into cattail marsh edgeCall: none heardPlumage: alternate
